stories.title: Erdoğan launches crackdown ahead of NATO summit
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Turkish authorities have carried out hundreds of arrests, banned protests and curbed media freedoms ahead of the upcoming NATO summit in Ankara, with campaigners accusing the government of using the gathering as a pretext for a clampdown on civil society. On Tuesday, Turkish police detained 225 people including academics, teachers and activists on suspicion of links to militant groups like the Islamic State — among them a 79-year-old environmental campaigner . The city of Ankara has a

stories.title: Germany joins push to delay EU methane rules
stories.summary: LUXEMBOURG — Berlin will seek to delay the rollout of key European Union rules regulating emissions of the potent greenhouse gas methane, joining a growing coalition of EU member countries arguing the regulation could jeopardize the bloc’s supply of oil and gas. It follows sustained lobbying against the rules by the United States government and the fossil fuel sector. “We urgently need a reworking of the methane regulation,” Katherina Reiche, Germany’s minister of economi

stories.title: Weather tracker: Heatwave expected to bring Germany and Poland to 40C
stories.summary: Austrian Grand Prix declares heat hazard as dramatic rise in temperatures forecast in central and northern Europe Temperatures are forecast to rise dramatically in parts of central and northern Europe this weekend as the intense heatwave continues . In Germany and Poland, highs up to or exceeding 40C (104F) are expected on both Saturday and Sunday, days after swathes of France experienced similar extreme temperatures. The Austrian Grand Prix, taking place this weekend in Spielberg, has declared

stories.title: Russia preparing possible ‘provocation’ in Baltic states or Poland, sources say
stories.summary: Kremlin may attempt to test Nato cohesion as Russia comes under growing pressure from Ukraine, according to sources from two countries Two countries on Nato’s eastern flank have warned that Russia is preparing a possible “provocation” in the Baltic states or Poland in an effort to test the cohesion of the western military alliance. Western sources also fear there could be danger on the horizon because the Kremlin is coming under pressure from Ukraine’s campaign of long-range attacks on targets n

stories.title: Archbishop of Canterbury calls for end to Israeli occupation of Palestine
stories.summary: Sarah Mullally ends visit to region with call for two-state solution that allows Israelis and Palestinians to live in peace The archbishop of Canterbury has called for an end to the Israeli occupation of Palestine after a pilgrimage in which she met Palestinians attacked by settlers and others detained without trial. Sarah Mullally, the head of the Church of England, and Hosam Naoum, the Anglican archbishop of Jerusalem, issued a joint letter on Thursday urging Anglicans around the world to pres

stories.title: Megszűnő szakmák, 200 jelentkezésből nulla ajánlat – hogyan találnak most munkát a fiatalok?
stories.summary: Szakemberek mellett húszas éveikben járó fiatalokkal beszélgettünk arról, hogy miért nehezebb most jó állást találni, mint pár éve, és igaz-e, hogy a Z generáció könnyebben mond fel, mint az idősebbek.

stories.title: Medián: A magyarok háromnegyede támogatná az euró bevezetését
stories.summary: A Fidesz szavazói között viszont sokkal többen látják úgy, hogy egyelőre nem kellene az euró bevezetésével foglalkoznunk.

stories.title: Guardian: Oroszország provokatív akciókra készülhet Lengyelországban vagy egy balti államban
stories.summary: Erre figyelmeztetett a héten két kelet-európai NATO tagállam titkosszolgálata is.

stories.title: Kéz a kézben a Fidesszel kérte a reklámszövetség a fővárosi képviselőket, ne fogadják el az óriásplakátok betiltásáról szóló javaslatot
stories.summary: Esküt tett két új fideszes és egy tiszás képviselő is a Fővárosi Közgyűlés pénteki ülésén. Bemutatkozott a budapesti rendőrfőkapitány.

stories.title: A közvetítői díj is visszajárna törölt járatnál egy új uniós alku szerint
stories.summary: A nem hivatalosan elfogadott rendelettel összehangolják a szabályokat a jegyközvetítőkre és a felügyelő hatóságokra.

stories.title: Ezermilliárdos lakossági befektetés mozdulhat meg a csökkenő infláció miatt
stories.summary: Az inflációkövető állampapíroknál sokkal kedvezőbb hozamokkal kecsegtetnek a fix kamatot ígérő konstrukciók, ha igaza lesz a Magyar Nemzeti Banknak az infláció alakulásával kapcsolatban.

stories.title: Orbán Anita külügyminiszter bejelentette, hogy 37 külképviselet vezetőjét hívja vissza
stories.summary: A változások egy része a lejáró megbízatásokhoz kapcsolódik. A külügyminiszter szerint a cél egy professzionális diplomáciai kar felépítése.

stories.title: Újra tartalékos katona lett Ruszin-Szendi Romulusz
stories.summary: A honvédelmi miniszter teljesítette az ejtőernyős egészségügyi alkalmassági vizsgálatot.

stories.title: Száz százalékban Jellinek Dániel cégéhez került az Auchan Magyarország
stories.summary: Az Indotek 2024-ben vásárolt először részesedést az Auchanban. A következő években több kisebb üzletet is terveznek nyitni az országban.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Ha belépünk az eurózónába, egy kormány sem teheti majd meg azt, amit Orbán Viktor tett
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök szerint az Orbán-kormány olyan volt, mint egy apuka, aki eljátssza a gyerek taníttatására félretett pénzt, ettől viszont megvéd az, ha bevezetjük az eurót.

stories.title: Az elmúlt évek változásait a kutatóknál már csak a kutatástámogatók szenvedték meg jobban
stories.summary: A Telexen megjelent véleménycikkében Hillier Dániel arról írt, hogy a magyar tudomány legnagyobb problémája nem a pénzhiány, hanem az intézményi bénultság: hiányzik az az adminisztratív háttér, amely enyhíthetné a kutatókra nehezedő bürokratikus terhelést. Szécsi Dorottya válaszcikkében megoldási javaslatokat tett erre a problémára: a modern vezetőképzés meghonosítását és az adminisztratív kutatássegítő szakemberek megbecsültségének növelését. Sorozatunk zárórészében Tamás Veronika mutat be egy

stories.title: Közel 150 év után politikai napilap nélkül marad Magyarország
stories.summary: A Népszava a Mediaworks keménykedésének esett áldozatul, a Magyar Nemzetet pedig augusztustól csak hetente jelenteti meg a kiadó. A kiegyezés óta először nem lesz tartósan nyomatott politikai napilap.

stories.title: Keressük a Balaton legolcsóbb és legdrágább lángosát
stories.summary: Egy jó lángosozás a nyár kihagyhatatlan része. Mi most az olvasóink segítségét kérjük, hogy tisztábban lássunk a magyar lángospiacon.

stories.title: Térképen, ahogy a hőkupola egyre inkább rátelepszik Magyarországra
stories.summary: Egyre durvul a helyzet, az extrém hőség jövő hét közepéig nem enged a szorításból. 40 fok fölötti hőmérsékletekre számítson mindenki.

stories.title: A baglyok nem tudják, mit kell tenni, amikor 130-cal érkezik egy fényes, egytonnás valami
stories.summary: A táj feldarabolódásában jelentős szerepet játszanak az egyre-másra épülő utak, amikben Magyarország élen jár. Elütött baglyok tömegei jelzik, hogy át kellett volna gondolni a gyorsforgalmi utak építését.

stories.title: Germany news: Major warship project to be scrapped
stories.summary: The Defense Ministry is dropping a multi-billion-euro project to build F126 frigates, media say. And train services have resumed after a communications glitch overnight halted Germany's rail network . DW has more.

stories.title: Poland, Ukraine: Dispute between two close allies deepens
stories.summary: Polish President Karol Nawrocki last week stripped Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy of Poland's highest state honor. How did it come to this? And what has it got to do with next year's election in Poland?

stories.title: Seal at Hungarian zoo brushes teeth
stories.summary: A seal at Nyíregyháza Zoo in Hungary brushes its teeth in the Arctic Ice World exhibit, showcasing daily care and animal enrichment activities.

stories.title: Trump finds partner in supreme court in his war against immigration
stories.summary: Recent rulings have bolstered president’s pitiless campaign against people of colour fleeing violence and disaster In the US supreme court, it seems Donald Trump has found loyalists in his crusade against immigration and immigrants. In a pair of rulings on Thursday, the court allowed the Trump administration to end humanitarian protections that have granted people from Haiti and Syria the right to live and work legally in the US for more than a decade, and cleared way for the government to turn

stories.title: EU Commission proposes excluding fighting-age men from Ukraine refugee scheme
stories.summary: The European Commission on Friday proposed extending temporary protection status for individuals fleeing the war in Ukraine until March 2028 — but excluding newly arrived men of fighting age. The temporary protection scheme offers more than 4 million people who fled Ukraine the right to live, work and study in EU countries. The scheme has been extended several times since the start of Russia’s full-scale invasion. Barring another extension, it will expire in March 2027. Under the Commissio

stories.title: VW reportedly looks to ax 100,000 jobs amid financial woes
stories.summary: Germany’s biggest automaker Volkswagen plans to cut about 15 percent of jobs from its workforce of more than 650,000, according to a Friday report from Manager Magazin business magazine. VW had previously announced it would cut about 50,000 jobs by 2030. Friday’s report says CEO Oliver Blume intends to double that figure, close four factories and spin off the VW brand. The automotive giant has been struggling financially, reporting a 14.3 percent drop in operating profit in the first quart

stories.title: Why Europe’s heat waves are still so deadly
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Once the heat dissipates, Europe will start counting its dead.  The extraordinary temperatures sweeping the western part of the continent this week will have killed hundreds, likely thousands, more than two decades after the deadly summer of 2003 alerted governments to the dangers of extreme heat.  While a first estimate of the death toll will take weeks, Spanish researchers are already attributing more than 210 fatalities between Sunday and Wednesday to the heat.

stories.title: Matthew Guy demands Moira Deeming apologise for assault claim after Victoria police find ‘no offence detected’
stories.summary: ‘There was no ambiguity. I did not do what was alleged,’ Guy says of fellow state Liberal MP’s accusation Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Victorian Liberal MP Matthew Guy is demanding an apology from his colleague Moira Deeming after police closed their investigation into an allegation he assaulted her, finding there was “no offence detected”. A visibly emotional Guy addressed reporters outside state parliament o

stories.title: Home secretary restricts minister’s access to documents as she calls for his sacking
stories.summary: Row between Shabana Mahmood and Mike Tapp broke out after he wrote unauthorised article about migration UK politics live – latest updates The migration minister, Mike Tapp, is to be denied access to sensitive documents and meetings without approval from the home secretary, as she called for the prime minister to sack him. Shabana Mahmood has demanded that Tapp be fired for writing an unauthorised article in the Times calling for overseas care workers to be exempt from changes to the immigration

stories.title: North Korea: Kim Jong Un oversees ballistic missile tests, calls for stronger 'offensive posture'
stories.summary: Pyongyang tested "major weapons" as North Korea's leader called for greater strike capabilities along the border with South Korea.

stories.title: Sudan: Fears of atrocities as RSF surrounds key city
stories.summary: As Sudan's paramilitary Rapid Support Forces mass around the strategic city of el-Obeid, fears are growing of atrocities similar to those seen in Darfur's el-Fasher. Could sanctions still prevent bloodshed?

stories.title: Montenegro arrests Iran-linked hacker wanted by US
stories.summary: The FBI was helping the Montenegrin police detain an Iranian-Turkish national whose cyberattacks on US infrastructure allegedly caused billions in damages.

stories.title: Supreme Court allows Trump to end protected status for Haitian and Syrian immigrants
stories.summary: The ruling opens the path for the Trump administration to deport hundreds of thousands of immigrants who have been living in the US for years.

stories.title: US sanctions Rwanda gold refinery accused of smuggling DR Congo's minerals
stories.summary: The US alleges Gasabo Gold Refinery smuggled at least 60kg of gold in early 2026.

stories.title: Paris desperate for ice amid record-breaking heat
stories.summary: PARIS — A historic heat wave has forced the city of Paris to turn toward big business to provide its firefighters with the resources they need. Officials from center-left Mayor Emmanuel Grégoire’s cabinet have been reaching out to large-scale food retailers to mobilize stocks over fears that the Parisian pompiers , who in France are often the first responders in case of emergencies, would run out of ice cubes needed to cool down victims of heat stroke. The plea was confirmed by two industr

stories.title: Tracing one delicious snack around the Mediterranean showed me that modern borders are absurd | Federico De Blasi
stories.summary: Migration and cultural exchange have always been the norm between coastal European and African nations. We should celebrate this shared history We are used to mapping the world by continents, dividing the globe into rigid geopolitical blocks. But to understand the complex reality behind each border, we are better off using a different, edible kind of cartography. For most of human existence, the Mediterranean has existed as an intercultural entity in its own right, where peoples and languages fr

stories.title: South Korea to train half a million military personnel to become ‘drone warriors’
stories.summary: All branches of the military will be taught how to use technology that has become a ‘game changer on the battlefield’, says defence minister All of South Korea’s military forces will be trained as drone operators in a sweeping overhaul of its warfare strategy, the defence minister has said. “All soldiers should be able to use drones like a second personal firearm,” Ahn Gyu-back, who heads the defence ministry in Seoul, said on Friday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Outrage as woman jailed for three years after criticising Somali government online
stories.summary: Sentencing of 27-year-old Sadia Moalim Ali condemned by former president and prime ministers as well as rights groups A rickshaw driver in Somalia has been sentenced to three years in prison for comments she made on social media, in a case that has caught the public’s attention and provoked outrage in the country. Sadia Moalim Ali, a 27-year-old nursing graduate, was originally charged with insulting government institutions and incitement to commit a crime, but convicted only of the former. Her

stories.title: Spain's military firefighters battle wildfire in Huesca province – video
stories.summary: Firefighters worked to halt a wildfire near Tamarite de Litera in Huesca province, where flames have scorched at least 1,000 hectares (2,470 acres). Firefighters from the military emergency unit worked through Thursday night to contain the blaze, clearing vegetation and using hoses to prevent the fire from spreading. The Aragon regional government declared a level 2 emergency on Thursday. Hundreds of people were evacuated from three municipalities as authorities sought to protect nearby communit

stories.title: Murderer of Henry Nowak shown in new footage lying about facing racist attack
stories.summary: Police bodycam footage also shows Vickrum Digwa claiming student was wounded from punch or falling over The murderer of Henry Nowak, the student whose death sparked riots in Southampton, is seen lying to police officers about being racially attacked in newly released bodyworn camera footage. Vickrum Digwa, 23, was jailed for life with a minimum of 20 years this month for stabbing the 18-year-old five times in December last year. Continue reading...

stories.title: Hawaii ruling praised by US gun rights groups – are more rollbacks coming?
stories.summary: Experts say decision presents problem for business owners – and indicates direction court’s conservatives are heading Experts say a supreme court ruling that struck down a Hawaii law banning people from carrying guns on private property without permission could present problems for business owners – and is a bellwether of what steps the majority-conservative court may take toward undoing policies they see as placing an undue burden on legal gun owners. The US supreme court handed down the ruling

stories.title: Ex-Trump adviser John Bolton expected to plead guilty in classified information case – US politics live
stories.summary: Bolton to plead guilty charges that he unlawfully retained sensitive national security information in agreement that includes $2.25m fine Sign up for the Breaking News US email In case you missed it yesterday, House speaker Mike Johnson said he was sending the bipartisan housing bill to Donald Trump ’s desk, a day after the president abruptly cancelled its signing as he tried to pressure the Senate to pass the unrelated Save America Act. Johnson told reporters after his lengthy crunch-time meeti

stories.title: The Guardian view on EU talks with the Taliban: selling out the rights of girls, women and other Afghans | Editorial
stories.summary: Five years after the fall of Kabul, European states are anxious to send migrants back – regardless of what it takes and what awaits them Days after Kabul fell to the Taliban in August 2021, the EU’s top diplomat stressed the need to protect women and girls. “Cooperation with any future Afghan government will be conditioned on … respect for the fundamental rights of all Afghans,” Josep Borrell pledged . The regime’s attack on women’s rights began immediately, and has only intensified. The Taliban

stories.title: India news: Government sends help to quake-hit Venezuela
stories.summary: Two air force planes carrying supplies as well as a field hospital unit are on their way. Also making headlines: Qatar has repatriated the bodies of four Indians killed in a gas explosion. DW has more.

stories.title: Montenegro police arrest Iranian accused of hacking US universities
stories.summary: The suspect is accused of helping steal data from more than 150 US universities over a hacking campaign dating back to 2013.

stories.title: Watch: South Korea holds first live-fire drill against drone swarms
stories.summary: South Korea's air force carried out its first live-fire exercise against a simulated swarm drone attack. Military forces used Vulcan air-defense guns, a portable laser and shotguns to intercept all 50 drones during the drill.

stories.title: Von der Leyen to visit Azerbaijan and Armenia to build on EU engagement
stories.summary: The European Commission president will visit Baku and Yerevan to build on the EU’s strategic engagement with the South Caucasus and to strengthen energy cooperation, trade and regional connectivity for the EU economies.

stories.title: US Supreme Court clears way for mass deportations of Haitians and Syrians
stories.summary: The 6-3 ruling hands Donald Trump a sweeping victory on immigration, stripping legal protections from hundreds of thousands of people the State Department warns would be returning to dangerous countries.

stories.title: EU Commission HQ forced to shut down air-conditioning amid heatwave
stories.summary: The European Commission’s headquarters was forced to shut down its air-conditioning system on Friday due to the heat wave. Staff working at the Berlaymont building received a text at midday, reading: “BERL — URGENT — Due to extreme weather conditions, forced shut down of air cooling system from floor 1 to 7 for the rest of the day.” The 13-story building is home to Commission President Ursula von der Leyen, her 26 commissioners and about 3,000 staff. Von der Leyen works on the 13th floor,

stories.title: UN agency pauses ship evacuations through strait of Hormuz after vessel struck
stories.summary: International Maritime Organization says safety guarantees must be confirmed before ships can move again A United Nations agency has paused the evacuation of ships through the strait of Hormuz after the British military said a vessel was hit by a projectile off the coast of Oman following the passage of several tankers that used a route backed by the UN. The head of the UN’s International Maritime Organization said on Thursday that the plan to move stranded ships out of the Persian Gulf through

stories.title: Too hot for work: why extreme heat is a threat to Europe’s productivity
stories.summary: High temperatures make some workplaces dangerous, with economists warning disruption will dent growth Monique Mosley is used to sweltering conditions at the food factory in Yorkshire where she works, but June’s record-breaking heatwave has made conditions unbearable. “We make hot filled food products and it’s common that we see temperatures in the high 30s,” she said. “Thanks to our union, our employer is offering extra breaks, but not every workplace is the same.” The latest heatwave to grip th

stories.title: Porsche magnate puts historic Salzburg villa up for sale after row over private ‘tunnel for one’
stories.summary: Plans by Wolfgang Porsche to bore private 500-metre road link through Austrian hill caused anger among locals Wolfgang Porsche, the Austrian-German automotive magnate, appears to have abandoned plans to build a private 500-metre tunnel for his cars through the Salzburg hills after a public uproar over the “tunnel for one”. In 2020, Porsche bought a storied 17th-century villa on the outskirts of Salzburg for €8.4m (£7.2m), and last autumn he secured permission from the city authorities for an est

stories.title: Australians to pay at least 20% more for iPads and Macbooks after Apple hikes prices citing AI
stories.summary: Apple Macbook 13-inch prices jumps from $1,799 to $2,099 while the cost of iPads surges by 25% Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australians woke up on Friday to more expensive Macbooks and iPads after Apple hiked prices worldwide, blaming an AI-driven cost-crunch for computer parts. The iPhone range was unaffected but experts predict Apple will raise prices for its flagship product later this year. Microsoft also

stories.title: Child malnutrition in Nepal has reached ‘alarming’ levels since aid cuts, survey finds
stories.summary: Fears hard-won gains in reducing child mortality over 20 years are at risk after end of USAID funding for nutrition programmes Child malnutrition in Nepal has reached “alarming” levels, according to the largest ever survey of under-fives in the country. The new figures came just over a year after USAID, the former US flagship agency closed by the Trump administration in 2025, stopped funding work on child nutrition in Nepal. Continue reading...

stories.title: Mexico’s new UK ambassador from ‘party of poor’ has 10 houses and £1m of jewellery
stories.summary: Alejandro Gertz Manero’s cars and properties contrast starkly with Morena party’s long association with austerity With his million-dollar jewellery collection and his two Rolls-Royces, Mexico’s new ambassador to the UK will fit right in with the Mayfair crowd. Former attorney general Alejandro Gertz Manero was appointed to the post by President Claudia Sheinbaum last year, but only recently disclosed his financial assets . Continue reading...

stories.title: Park to protect 12,000 koalas to go ahead via contentious carbon credit deal by Albanese government
stories.summary: Minns government waited on federal approval of carbon credit scheme before proceeding with long-awaited great koala national park Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A long-promised New South Wales great koala national park is set to go ahead after the Albanese government greenlit the state to receive hundreds of millions of dollars for protecting native forests previously earmarked for logging. The assistant climate

stories.title: Heathrow expects fall in passengers and profits this year because of Iran war
stories.summary: Airport has also been ‘engaging closely’ with regulator to discuss cost of plan to build third runway Business live – latest updates Heathrow has said that passenger numbers and profits will fall this year because of the war in the Middle East. Europe’s busiest airport said it expected a 1.1% decline in the total number of passengers to 83.6 million as the Iran war affects air travel. Continue reading...

stories.title: VW plans to cut up to 10,000 jobs and shut plants, report says
stories.summary: German firm reportedly considering doubling previously announced staff reductions amid Chinese competition Business live – latest updates Germany’s Volkswagen is to cut up to 100,000 jobs and reduce and eventually stop production at some plants, according to reports. The company has refused to comment on reports of a management presentation at a board meeting outlining dramatic cost cutting, but if it goes ahead it would mean Volkswagen doubling previously announced staff reductions. Continue re

stories.title: Whereabouts of nearly 300 people with Ebola unknown in DR Congo
stories.summary: Fears over ‘huge community transmission’ as modelling predicts thousands of deaths in DRC by September The whereabouts of almost 300 people who have tested positive for Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo is unknown, according to Africa’s top public health official. The humanitarian crisis amid the conflict in the affected areas means more than 1 million people are living in camps to which health workers have no access, Dr Jean Kaseya, director general of the Africa Centers for Disease

stories.title: ‘Chock full of incredible animals’: marine expedition uncovers 31 new species in two weeks
stories.summary: Experts worked in ocean midwater off Brazil at near-record speeds thanks to cutting-edge tech A marine biology expedition in international waters off the coast of Brazil has discovered 31 new species in just two weeks. The researchers believe the speed at which the species were found and identified may be a record, in part because of the cutting-edge technology designed and built by the science and engineering team. For the first time on board a ship, the researchers were able to observe the liv

stories.title: Paris bans drinking alcohol in public as hospitals hit heatwave breaking point
stories.summary: City’s Pride march also postponed as police chief says health and emergency services have been overwhelmed Europe live – latest updates Drinking takeaway alcohol in the street has been banned in Paris this weekend and the city’s pride march has been cancelled to spare overwhelmed ambulance services and overcrowded hospitals in the deadly heatwave . “As you know, drinking alcohol with the sun beating down can have a devastating effect,” the ⁠Paris police chief, Patrice Faure, ​told ​BFM TV. Conti

stories.title: New study suggests health damage from exposure to Ohio toxic train spill
stories.summary: Research finds immune systems still ‘responding to toxic chemical exposures’ related to East Palestine derailment In the weeks after the East Palestine train wreck culminated in a towering fireball and chemical release in the small Ohio town, Jessica Boersma was seriously exposed to the stew of compounds emitted from the derailment site. Boersma lives less than a quarter mile away, and, as a city council member, she had to spend significant time at the site coordinating with first responders. Co

stories.title: ‘Everyone is talking about Cape Verde’: World Cup run delights diaspora community in UK
stories.summary: Cape Verdeans in Britain feeling ‘incredibly proud’ after team’s hard-fought draws against Spain and Uruguay World Cup live – latest updates For as long as she can remember, 13-year-old Lauryn struggled to find a map that included Cape Verde. Now, to her great delight, the tiny African island nation is finally centre stage. “Seeing our country shown across the world at the World Cup makes me feel incredibly proud,” Lauryn says. “After the first match, everyone was talking about Cape Verde. Peopl

stories.title: Kent van driver praised for giving lift to armed officer chasing suspect
stories.summary: Bodycam footage captures moment motorist pulls over to help police officer and calls: ‘Get in the back’ A van driver in Kent has been praised after giving a lift to an armed police officer who was chasing a suspect. Bodycam footage from the officer captured the moment the motorist pulled over to help the police on 16 June and called out: “Get in the back. Get in the back.” Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump administration moves to restart LGBTQ+ suicide hotline it initially ended
stories.summary: The Trevor Project non-profit that helped pioneer LGBTQ+ ‘press 3’ option for 988 hotline is being shut out as it restarts The Trump administration is moving to restart the specialized LGBTQ+ option for youth who contact the 988 crisis intervention hotline – but the group that helped pioneer the idea is being shut out. The Trevor Project, the New York-based leading non-profit for suicide prevention in LGBTQ+ young people in the US, may not be allowed to offer the service it had helped develop fo

stories.title: Europe heatwave live: UK June heat record broken for third day in a row; France and Belgium cancel events
stories.summary: Estimated 150 million people in Europe could experience temperatures above 35C today European heatwave is worst ever and impossible without climate crisis, scientists say Over in the UK, firefighters are still trying to bring a large wildfire in Derbyshire under control. The blaze, which has burned over 500 square metres of moorland and woodland on Tintwistle Moor, near Glossop, broke out on Wednesday evening, with fire crews from Manchester and Derbyshire deploying a water-dropping helicopter a

stories.title: Billionaire Leon Black to appear before House panel over ties to Jeffrey Epstein
stories.summary: Scrutiny of Black’s association with Epstein has intensified after DoJ released millions of files last year and this year The billionaire financier Leon Black is scheduled to appear before the House committee on oversight and reform on Friday morning as part of its investigation into Jeffrey Epstein . The interview will be conducted behind closed doors, though the committee is expected to release a transcript at a later date, as it has done with previous interviews. Continue reading...

stories.title: Why is alcohol dangerous in a heatwave, and should I cut it out completely?
stories.summary: Those partial to a pint may be relieved to know a modest of amount of weak beer may actually be beneficial European heatwave live – latest updates As Europe endures a record-breaking heatwave, countries are taking steps to keep people safe and prevent health services from becoming overstretched. Parisians face a temporary ban on drinking alcohol in public to reduce the pressure on the hospitals after a four-fold rise in cardiac arrests in a 24-hour period. We look at why drinking alcohol can be

stories.title: ‘Fork in the road’: CEO of Amazon-backed Rivian on why carmakers need to invest in EVs
stories.summary: RJ Scaringe says firms focused on selling fossil fuel engines risk being ‘woefully behind’ on technology by end of decade Carmakers that focus on selling fossil fuel engines are at risk of being “woefully behind” on technology by the end of the decade, according to the boss of Rivian, an Amazon-backed US electric carmaker. RJ Scaringe, Rivian’s founder and chief executive, said the car industry has reached a “fork in the road” in the choice between short-term profits and the heavy investments, p

stories.title: Venezuela earthquakes: 589 confirmed dead so far as international rescue teams arrive – latest updates
stories.summary: ‘We hope to rescue as many living people as possible’, says the acting president, Delcy Rodríguez, but authorities fear the death toll will be in the thousands China’s president Xi Jinping said Beijing was ready to provide Venezuela with “disaster relief and reconstruction” assistance. Xi sent a message of condolence to Venezuela’s interim president Delcy Rodríguez today, according to Chinese state news agency Xinhua. Continue reading...

stories.title: Oil price falls to pre-Iran war levels as more tankers exit strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Stock markets on both sides of Atlantic up as concerns ease over prospect of another inflationary shock Business live – latest updates Oil prices have fallen to pre-Iran war levels as more oil tankers exited the strait of Hormuz. Brent crude, the global benchmark, fell to a low of $72.24 a barrel on Thursday, slightly lower than the day before the US and Israel launched missile attacks on Tehran on 28 February. Prices have fallen more than 20% this month. Continue reading...

stories.title: Xi Jinping has hosted more than a dozen leaders this year, as ‘middle powers’ look beyond the US
stories.summary: China’s leader wants to promote his alternative to the current world order, and his efforts are being assisted by a capricious US Xi Jinping meets Bangladesh’s new prime minister on Friday, the latest in a wave of world leaders to visit Beijing this year as the Chinese leader builds his influence and economic ties, and seeks to “shift the balance of power” away from the west. Xi’s meeting with Tarique Rahman comes less than two weeks after the Chinese leader welcomed Myanmar’s military chief-tur

stories.title: Rescue teams race to Venezuela amid fears thousands killed in earthquakes
stories.summary: US among countries sending help to search for survivors on north coast, where dozens of buildings flattened, as official death toll reaches 235 Rescue teams are racing to Venezuela’s shattered northern coast after almost simultaneous earthquakes reduced dozens of buildings to rubble, killing at least 235 people but with thousands more fatalities feared. Officials said at least 4,300 people were injured as rescue missions continue. The US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, said the defence departme

stories.title: Europe heat wave 'virtually impossible' without human impact
stories.summary: A new study says Europe's current heat wave would have been nearly impossible without human-driven climate change. And Germany is expecting things to warm up even more. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Magdeburg Christmas market attacker sentenced to life in prison
stories.summary: A Magdeburg court has sentenced a 51-year-old to life over his 2024 attack on a Christmas market in the city. The trained psychiatrist drove an SUV through a barricade and into a crowd, killing six and injuring hundreds.

stories.title: Here's what southern Europeans know about surviving heat waves
stories.summary: People in countries like Greece, Italy and Spain have long lived with high temperatures. Here's what more northerly countries can learn from them about managing heat waves as they become more common.

stories.title: Germany news: Berlin's orchestra eases dress code amid heat
stories.summary: Men are not obliged to wear jackets during the orchestra's end-of-season performance this weekend. Meanwhile, a court has sentenced the defendent in the Magdeburg Christmas market attack to life. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Singapore: French teen in trouble over straw-licking video
stories.summary: A French student is accused of licking a straw at a juice vending machine before putting it back and posting the video online. He faces jail time if convicted in Singapore, a country famous for its tough laws.

stories.title: Fact check: As Europe bakes, should you trust your sunscreen?
stories.summary: As a heatwave sweeps across Europe, claims are resurfacing online questioning whether sunscreen is safe or linked to skin cancer risk.

stories.title: Japan needs more foreign workers, but many feel unwelcome
stories.summary: While Japan faces a deepening labor shortage and growing demand for foreign workers, it has been moving to tighten visa rules and regulations.

stories.title: Flights disrupted in Japan as 2 tropical storms move in
stories.summary: Two storms were set to converge above Japan, prompting officials to issue landslide warnings and evacuation orders for hundreds of thousands of people.

stories.title: Balochistan activist's life sentence raises fear of unrest
stories.summary: With the conviction of a leading rights campaigner, Pakistan has drawn criticism from rights groups and sparked fears it could deepen unrest in Balochistan and weaken trust in peaceful activism.

stories.title: Bayer: Why so many Roundup weedkiller lawsuits?
stories.summary: Bayer was sued for failing to add cancer warnings to its controversial pesticide Roundup, when US regulators opposed adding one. DW asks if the latest Supreme Court win will stop the chemical giant from paying billions.

stories.title: Wagner festival reinstates Holocaust memorial event after cancellation
stories.summary: The Bayreuth Festival had canceled a planned memorial lecture featuring Jewish author and broadcaster Michel Friedman, drawing sharp criticism.

stories.title: World Cup: 'Pride Match' sparks objections in Egypt and Iran
stories.summary: Friday night's game in Seattle was designated as a "Pride Match" well ahead of the World Cup draw. The fixture list then threw up Egypt vs. Iran, two countries in which homosexuality is criminalized.

stories.title: Death toll from Venezuela earthquakes rises to 589, search and rescue efforts continue
stories.summary: The two quakes struck in quick succession, just minutes apart, according to the US Geological Survey, marking it one of the South American nation’s worst disasters in over a century.

stories.title: Gulf states weigh living side by side with Iran as Tehran weaponises Hormuz
stories.summary: As the frontline countries paying the largest price for the Iran war, pressed by severe economic urgencies, the Gulf states are beginning their own firm process towards regional stability with rival Iran, while Tehran continues weaponising the Strait of Hormuz to sow division with its neighbours.

stories.title: Aerial footage reveals destruction in coastal Venezuela
stories.summary: Multi-storey buildings have collapsed in La Guaira following two major earthquakes on Wednesday.

stories.title: Reflecting Pool liner cut with sharp knife or razor, National Park Service says
stories.summary: Earlier in the week Donald Trump blamed vandals for "a 300 foot long gash" in the pool and accused someone of putting fertiliser in the water.

stories.title: Japan fans flood Shibuya as World Cup draw with Sweden sets up Brazil clash
stories.summary: Japan reach World Cup last 32 after 1-1 draw with Sweden on 26 June, prompting celebrations across Tokyo. , sparking huge celebrations in Tokyo’s Shibuya. They now face Brazil in the knockout stage.

stories.title: Historic joy in Abidjan as Ivory Coast reach World Cup knockouts for first time
stories.summary: Ivory Coast reaches World Cup knockout stage for the first time after Nicolas Pépé’s brace beats Curaçao 2-0, sparking huge celebrations in Abidjan.

stories.title: Asia stock markets slide as tech shares slump
stories.summary: Trading on South Korea's Kospi index was halted for the third time this week to prevent panic selling.

stories.title: Venezuela earthquakes: bodies recovered as rescue efforts continue in La Guaira
stories.summary: Rescue teams worked through the ruins of collapsed buildings in La Guaira on 25 June after twin earthquakes devastated northern Venezuela, killing at least 188 people and injuring around 1,500.

stories.title: Iraq: Ashoura in Karbala draws thousands of Shiite pilgrims for rituals
stories.summary: Thousands of Shiite pilgrims mark Ashoura in Karbala, mourning Imam Hussein’s 680 AD death with processions, prayers and reinforced security.

stories.title: Housing board votes to freeze New York rents in victory for Mamdani
stories.summary: The decision was welcomed by many tenants - but landlords fear it will worsen housing conditions.

stories.title: Moment woman pulled from rubble alive after Venezuela earthquake
stories.summary: First responders and volunteers pulled a survivor from under the rubble of a collapsed building in La Guaira.

stories.title: The abundant but expensive energy source that's under your feet
stories.summary: Start-ups are taking fresh approaches to geothermal energy, but will the economics work?

stories.title: Senior Ukrainian intelligence official jailed for life for spying for Russia
stories.summary: Col Dmytro Kozyura agreed to share state secrets and systematically disclosed classified information, prosecutors say.

stories.title: Christmas market attacker jailed for life for murdering six in Germany
stories.summary: A nine-year-old and five women were killed when Taleb Al-Abdulmohsen drove into the market in 2024.

stories.title: UN pauses Strait of Hormuz evacuation plan after cargo ship attacked
stories.summary: The ship was reportedly struck by an "unknown projectile" near Oman but no casualties were reported.

stories.title: Lebanon: thousands mark Ashoura in Nabatieh amid rubble left by Israeli strikes
stories.summary: Thousands of Shiite Muslims marked Ashoura in Nabatieh on 26 June, holding processions through neighbourhoods devastated by recent Israeli airstrikes in a powerful display of resilience.

stories.title: Canada eligible to join Eurovision competition
stories.summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney raised the idea of Canada joining the song contest in his 2025 budget.

stories.title: Cargo ship hit by projectile in Hormuz as Iran issues renewed passage warning
stories.summary: The British military said a cargo ship on the new Omani UN-backed route sustained bridge damage from a projectile, hours after the IRGC warned that vessels without Iranian permission were transiting "illegally".

stories.title: What Keir Starmer's resignation means for European security
stories.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer was widely known for his support of Ukraine and his efforts towards a new EU–UK defense partnership. What will happen to those policies now that Starmer has announced his resignation?

stories.title: Britain’s six prime ministers since 2016 – ranked!
stories.summary: From Cameron’s Brexit exit to Starmer’s Burnham bow-out, half a dozen PMs have gone. So who’s the best of the bunch? The UK has had six prime ministers in the last 10 years – with a seventh likely to be in place by as early as mid-July. John Crace ranks those who have been booted out of Downing Street between 2016 and 2026. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Constitutional coup’ claims as Zimbabwe senate approves extending presidential term
stories.summary: Opposition figures fear changes will further tighten 83-year-old president Emmerson Mnangagwa’s hold on power Zimbabwe is on the brink of amending its constitution to give the president more time in office, a change that the government says will bring stability but that opponents have labelled a “constitutional coup”. The upper house of Zimbabwe’s parliament voted on Wednesday 75-4 in favour of the constitutional amendments, which will allow President Emmerson Mnangagwa to stay in office until 2

stories.title: Israeli forces arrest Palestinian ‘doctor of the poor’
stories.summary: Dr Mazen Al-Rantisi, a 71-year-old physician well known for providing care to low-income Palestinians, was arrested in the occupied West Bank Israeli forces on Sunday arrested a prominent 71-year-old Palestinian physician known as the “doctor of the poor” in a pre-dawn raid on his home in the occupied West Bank, prompting widespread condemnation. Dr Mazen Al-Rantisi, a physician widely known for providing care to low-income Palestinians, was arrested in the al-Tira neighbourhood of Ramallah. Con

stories.title: Iran rejects UN-backed plan to free ships trapped in strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Blocking of proposal backed by Oman signals new threat to free passage through strait vital to world economy Iran has rejected UN-backed plans for the mass evacuation of ships through the strait of Hormuz, creating a new threat to the free passage of commercial ships through the strait. The proposal, backed by Oman, was potentially the first phase of a broader Omani proposal to consult on setting up a new management of the strait based on voluntary fees and modelled on the Malacca and Singapore

stories.title: New evidence casts doubt on RFK Jr testimony before Senate
stories.summary: Kennedy repeatedly said 2019 Samoa trip had ‘nothing to do with vaccines’. An email from his then colleague says they were on a vaccine-related ‘mission’ New evidence has emerged that Robert F Kennedy Jr was on a vaccine-related “mission” when he visited Samoa ahead of a deadly measles outbreak in 2019, raising further questions about whether the US health secretary lied to the US Senate when he said the trip had “nothing to do with vaccines”. Records obtained by the Guardian show Kennedy’s coll

stories.title: The 'Flamingo Revolution': What's behind Albania's protests?
stories.summary: For weeks now, images of crowds of Albanians protesting on the streets have been relayed around the world. The protesters say they are fighting for democracy. PM Edi Rama insists the movement is part of a hybrid war.

stories.title: Banning social media for teens: Solution or overreaction?
stories.summary: One in seven teens in the European Union spends more than eight hours a day in front of screens, a new study shows. Are social media bans the answer?

stories.title: Germany considers 'opt-out' organ donor option
stories.summary: Germany is one of few European countries that still has an exclusively "opt-in" choice for organ donation — despite long waiting lists for organs. Now, parliament is debating a change.

stories.title: Young people avoiding the news, Reuters report
stories.summary: An alarming number of young people are turning away from the news, according to the Reuters Digital News Report presented at DW's Global Media Forum. Despite this, the report's author says, "journalism still matters."

stories.title: US Supreme Court sides with Bayer over Roundup cancer suits
stories.summary: Bayer, which acquired Roundup in 2018, said the decision is good for science and farmers. The Supreme Court's ruling was met with backlash from both Republicans and Democrats.

stories.title: Germany disappoints in loss to Ecuador at 2026 World Cup
stories.summary: Germany had already qualified for the knockouts of the 2026 World Cup, but their performance was still disappointing in a 2-1 defeat.

stories.title: King Charles won't live in Buckingham Palace, reveals taxes
stories.summary: Buckingham Palace has been the British monarch's official residence in London for nearly two centuries. But royal officials say King Charles and Queen Camilla won't reside there.

stories.title: Death toll from twin earthquakes in Venezuela has more than doubled to 589, acting President Rodriguez says. Follow live
stories.summary: Acting President Delcy Rodriguez said at least 589 people have died after two strong earthquakes rattled the capital and surrounding regions. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Oil price falls back to pre-Iran war levels
stories.summary: Signs that traffic through the key Strait of Hormuz shipping route is gradually resuming has helped to push the oil price down.

stories.title: Families lay flowers on barbed wire barricade on anniversary of deadly Kenya protests
stories.summary: Protesters demand justice for the dozens killed during the 2024 demonstrations and last year's anniversary protests.

stories.title: Could a Madison Square Garden wedding be the love story of Taylor Swift's wildest dreams?
stories.summary: Officials confirmed someone applied for a permit to close the streets near Madison Square Garden on 4 July.

stories.title: Women alleging rape and sexual assault in France call to abolish statute of limitations
stories.summary: More than 50 women are calling for the abolition of the statute of limitations which they say has prevented them seeking justice.

stories.title: A Chinese box office hit sparks a debate about identity in Singapore
stories.summary: A nostalgic tale about family, hope and hardship has opened an unexpected conversation.

stories.title: Why the trail went cold in Nancy Guthrie case
stories.summary: The lack of a breakthrough in the abduction of a famous TV anchor's mother is puzzling given the huge publicity.

stories.title: Paris restricts alcohol consumption and sales as Europe's heatwave shifts east
stories.summary: Temperatures in Germany could hit 40C in some areas while French officials bring in alcohol restrictions to ease pressure on hospitals.

stories.title: Row over alleged theft of donations from India's landmark Ram temple
stories.summary: Questions are being raised over the handling of cash, valuable jewellery, gold and silver offered by devotees.

stories.title: Rescues and prayers a day after Venezuelan earthquakes
stories.summary: The BBC's Vanessa Silva reports from Caracas as the city faces the aftermath of back-to-back tremors.

stories.title: Earthquake is devastating blow to Venezuela at time of uncertainty
stories.summary: The twin quakes come less than six months after Venezuela's then-leader Nicolás Maduro was seized by US forces.

stories.title: 98. percben szerzett góllal Törökország 3-2-re legyőzte a már csoportelső USA-t
stories.summary: A törökök szépen és eredményesen búcsúztak a vb-től, az amerikaiak Bosznia ellen folytatják.

stories.title: Paraguay és Ausztrália bemutatta, amitől előre lehetett félni a vb-csoportkörnél
stories.summary: Egyik csapat sem hajtotta ki magát, egy nagyon gyenge meccs végén kiegyeztek a 0-0-val.

stories.title: A videóbíró sehol nem volt, pedig nem szabadott volna megadni a németek gólját Ecuador ellen
stories.summary: Aleksandar Pavlović közvetlenül a gól előtt emelte olyan magasra a lábát, hogy fejen is rúgta az ecuadori Pedro Vitét.

stories.title: MTA-elnök: Az előző rendszer nem tűrt semmilyen jelentős autonómiát
stories.summary: Pósfai Mihály szerint rendkívül fontos, hogy az MTA-nak együttműködésen alapuló kapcsolata legyen a mindenkori kormánnyal.

stories.title: Két tunéziai játékos is elkésett a második félidőről, a holland kapitány Koeman kiakadt
stories.summary: Világbajnokságon ritkán látható pillanatok.

stories.title: Eltévedt egy külföldi turista a visegrádi erdőben, konferenciahívással sikerült megtalálni
stories.summary: A nő telefonján nem volt internet, és papíralapú térképet sem vitt magával.

stories.title: 38 fok is lehet pénteken, és az igazi hőkupola még csak ezután jön
stories.summary: Forróság lesz az egész országban, bár a tavalyi rekord pénteken nem dől meg.

stories.title: Török Gábor Sulyok elmozdításáról: Határátlépés történik az egymondatos alkotmánymódosítással
stories.summary: Az elemző szerint egy versengő politikai rendszerben a politikai teljesítmény az, ha legitim eszközökkel érem el a lemondásokat.

stories.title: Két nagy vb-rekord is megdőlt az éjjel
stories.summary: Mindkettőnek köze van az új, 48 csapatos lebonyolításhoz.

stories.title: A hőség miatt elhalasztják a lyoni Pride-ot
stories.summary: A Franciaországban tomboló hőség miatt szeptemberre halasztják az eseményt.

stories.title: Az ecuadori kapitány a nézők közé mászva ünnepelt, az elnök nemzeti ünnepnapot rendelt el a vb-továbbjutás után
stories.summary: A Curaçao elleni döntetlen után még Beccacece családtagjait vegzálták az eredmény miatt, most mindenki őt ünnepelte.

stories.title: Nagy szükség volna rá, eddig mégis alig foglalkozott az oktatáspolitika a kora gyerekkori fejlesztéssel
stories.summary: Az Oktatási és Gyermekügyi Minisztériumban külön államtitkárságot kapott a kora gyerekkori jóllét. Sok gyerek nem jut hozzá a korai fejlesztéshez, és a bölcsődében, óvodában dolgozók is kevés támogatást kapnak. Milyen változások várhatók ezen a területen, és miért annyira fontos a kora gyerekkor időszaka? Utánajártunk.

stories.title: Meghirdetése után órákkal meg is indult az ukránok tömeges dróntámadása orosz célpontok ellen
stories.summary: Az eddigi egyik legnagyobb dróntámadást hajtotta végre Ukrajna orosz célpontok ellen. A lendület ellenére azonban egyre nehezebb az ukrán védők helyzete Donyeck megye utolsó erődítése körül.

stories.title: Ötszáz méter hosszú privát alagutat akart építeni Ferdinand Porsche unokája, a helyiek megfúrták a projektet
stories.summary: Pedig már az engedély is megvolt.

stories.title: A világ 400 legerősebb vállalatába került az OTP a Forbes listája szerint
stories.summary: Négy év alatt több mint 600 helyet javított a listán a regionális bankcsoporttá növekedett OTP.

stories.title: Százmillió forint fölött is kaszálhat a három NB I.-es csapat a vb-n szereplő játékosai után
stories.summary: A Fradinál már látszik, mennyit érhet Lenny Joseph szereplése, az ETO-s Benbuali és a felcsúti Duarte után járó összeg még tovább nőhet.

stories.title: A céges autó több mint egy slusszkulcs és egy üzemanyagkártya (x)
stories.summary: A legtöbb cégnél nincs külön munkatárs a flottakezelésre. A szükséges adminisztráció mégis elkészül: valaki időpontot foglal a szervizbe, más intézi a biztosítást, egy harmadik követi a határidőket. Mire a feladatok összeadódnak, kiderül, hogy a cégesautó-kezelés és -üzemeltetés jelentős erőforrást köt le – csak éppen nehéz megmondani, kinél jelentkezik a terhelés.

stories.title: A kutyabarát munkahely ma már sokak számára alapelvárás (x)
stories.summary: A munkavállalói elvárások folyamatosan változnak, és mára jóval túlmutatnak a versenyképes fizetésen vagy a béren kívüli juttatásokon. A Mars Incorporated – olyan népszerű márkák gyártója, mint a PEDIGREE® vagy a WHISKAS® – legfrissebb kutatása szerint egyre fontosabb a munkavállalóknak az, hogy hogyan viszonyul a munkahelye a háziállatokhoz.

stories.title: Jubileumi fényáradat borítja be Pécset a 10. Zsolnay Fényfesztiválon (x)
stories.summary: Tíz évvel azután, hogy Pécs utcái először ragyogtak fel a kortárs fényművészet varázsában, a Zsolnay Fényfesztivál jubileumi, 10. kiadása minden eddiginél nagyobb és látványosabb. Július 2-5. között négy éjszakán át több mint harminc alkotással borítja fényárba a várost, ráadásul tematikájában Pécs világhírű szülötte, Victor Vasarely előtt tiszteleg a Vasarely-emlékév keretében.

stories.title: A hőkupola megolvasztja hétvégén Európát, több nagy rendezvényt lefújtak
stories.summary: Hollandia történetében először adtak ki hőség miatt vörös riasztást, Franciaországban a mentők súlyosan leterheltek.

stories.summary: Polgármesterként a fia és közeli ismerősei tulajdonában álló cégekkel kötött szerződést, a nyomozók szerint ezzel 102 millió forintos vagyoni hátrányt okozott az önkormányzatnak.

stories.title: Klopp kiosztotta a német válogatottat: A minőség mentalitás nélkül nem elég
stories.summary: Ecuador jobban akarta a sikert, mint a már továbbjutott németek.

stories.title: Mikor lehet francia–német vagy brazil–angol a foci-vb-n? Összejöhet egy USA–Irán? Mi kell a Messi–Ronaldo-meccshez?
stories.summary: Megannyi kínzó kérdés a foci-vb-n, amikor még csak a csoportok fele zárult le. De azért mégis körvonalazódik, milyen meccsek jöhetnek a 32-es táblán, ahonnan egyenes kieséses szakaszban folytatódik a vb. Segítünk eligazodni.

stories.title: Miért olyan iszonyatosan drága a limonádé?
stories.summary: A pár száz forintos alapanyagárú italok éttermi ára egyre inkább szíven üt, ez pedig különösen elgondolkodtató olyankor, mikor hőség van. Mi kerül 2300 forintba egy limonádén?

stories.title: Nyilvános III. Károly adóbevallása, a király több mint 50 milliárd forintnyi támogatást kap az államtól egy évre
stories.summary: III. Károly király bő ötmilliárd forintnyi adót fizetett, ezzel bekerült Nagy-Britannia top 100 adófizetője közé.

stories.summary: A fitneszguru egy praktizálástól már eltiltott orvos állításai alapján kutyáknak és macskáknak való féreghajtóval kezeli a feleségét. Megnéztük, mit mond a tudomány a két szer és a daganatkezelés kapcsolatáról.

stories.title: Majdnem 600-ra nőtt a venezuelai földrengés áldozatainak száma, több tízezer embert még mindig keresnek
stories.summary: A hivatalos bejelentőoldalakon 50 ezer eltűnt embert keresnek a rokonaik.

stories.title: Miért kéri Kapitány István, hogy ne légkondizzunk a kora esti órákban?
stories.summary: A rezsicsökkentés miatt a lakosságnak akkor sem drága az áram, ha csúcsidőben fogyaszt. Ám az ország áramrendszerét egyensúlyban tartó Mavirnak sokat segít az, aki a miniszter kérését megfogadja.

stories.title: Egymás után dőlnek meg a melegrekordok Angliában és Svájcban
stories.summary: Egyik országban sem mértek még soha júniusban olyan meleget, mint tegnap.

stories.title: Meghalt Putyin egykori erős embere
stories.summary: Szergej Ivanov volt védelmi miniszter, az elnöki hivatal helyettes vezetője, végül Putyin megbízottja. Karrierje az elnökéhez hasonlóan a leningrádi KGB-ből indult.

stories.title: Nagy tapssal fogadták el a fővárosi képviselők a plakáttilalomról szóló fővárosi javaslatot
stories.summary: A közgyűlés úgy döntött, drágulni fog a taxizás. Bemutatkozott az új rendőrfőkapitány, ahogy több fideszes és egy tiszás képviselő is.

stories.title: Távozik a Trafó igazgatója
stories.summary: Erdődi Katalin és a Fővárosi Önkormányzat közös megegyezéssel döntöttek az ügyvezetői megbízás megszüntetéséről.

stories.title: Rendkívüli állapot a Krímben, nehezen bírnak az ukrán drónokkal az orosz megszállók
stories.summary: Egyre kevésbé képes a háborút elindító Oroszország arra, hogy a megszállt területektől távol tartsa az ukrán csapásokat.

stories.title: Pakistan police rescue French woman and children allegedly held captive by husband for 12 years
stories.summary: Husband arrested after Sylvie Yasmina, 54, and five children found at home in north-western province Pakistan police say they have rescued a French woman and her five children after she told authorities she had been held captive by her husband for more than a decade and subjected to years of domestic abuse in the country’s north-west. The woman, identified as 54-year-old Sylvie Yasmina, was rescued earlier this week from a mud-brick home in Bara, a town in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province near the Af

stories.title: Reform UK plan to target EU nationals based in Britain ‘absolutely outrageous’
stories.summary: Exclusive: Rights group says Nigel Farage’s party is reneging on promises made during the Brexit referendum campaign UK politics live – latest updates EU nationals based permanently in the UK have expressed alarm over a Reform UK plan to target their rights to accommodation and employment, saying the policy is a betrayal of promises made in the Brexit referendum 10 years ago. Under updated migration policies, Nigel Farage’s party would evict all overseas nationals from social housing and make it

stories.title: Why is Germany taking a big stake in a major tankmaker?
stories.summary: The German government will buy a 40% stake in tankmaker KNDS. The deal is as much about control in the critical defense sector as it is about the Franco-German relationship.

stories.title: Fact check: No, Zelenskyy wasn't killed in a Russian airstrike
stories.summary: Amid a flare-up in violence between Russia and Ukraine, viral posts claim Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has died. DW Fact check takes a closer look.

stories.title: Ukraine says major Crimea bridge destroyed in latest attack
stories.summary: Ukraine continues to hit infrastructure on the Crimea to isolate the Russian-annexed peninsula. Meanwhile, Russia attacked Volodymyr Zelenskyy's hometown with a ballistic missile in a deadly strike.

stories.title: The ICE raids are over, but some Minnesotans say they still live in fear
stories.summary: Months after the Trump administration's immigration crackdown officially ended, Minneapolis is still feeling the impact.

stories.title: Two years after Gen Z uprising, Kenyan protesters return to Nairobi streets
stories.summary: Two years after Kenya's 2024 Gen Z tax revolt shook the nation, protesters gathered in Nairobi on 25 June to seek justice for victims and press for greater government accountability.

stories.title: Biggest city in Crimea without power after Ukraine strikes
stories.summary: Sevastopol's Moscow-installed governor warns there will be no electricity in some areas until the evening.

stories.title: Exploit last North Sea oil and gas or risk mass job losses, Andy Burnham urged
stories.summary: British Chamber of Commerce boss Shevaun Haviland says transition to clean energy could be handled better Andy Burnham should be ready to exploit the UK’s remaining North Sea oil and gas resources to avoid mass job losses in Scotland and the north-east, the director of the British Chambers of Commerce (BCC), Shevaun Haviland, has said. The decision about whether to allow extraction at the Jackdaw and Rosebank fields now appears likely to fall to a Burnham administration. Continue reading...

stories.title: Papua New Guinea at risk of food shortages as El Niño brings frost and drought
stories.summary: Oxfam predicts PNG will be worst-hit country in Pacific from the weather pattern, with up to 3 million people affected nationwide Families across Papua New Guinea’s Highlands are facing depleted harvests and the threat of hunger after the El Niño weather pattern brought frost and prolonged dry conditions that have destroyed food gardens providing sustenance and income for thousands of households. The effects of El Niño emerged in recent weeks, bringing drought conditions, falling water levels an

stories.title: Stubborn, arrogant, a genius: France’s De Gaulle epic shows up the tepidity of our politics | Alexander Hurst
stories.summary: The man who led Free France during Nazi occupation was single-minded to the point of obstinacy. But he believed in his own power to make history How much of our political agency have we sacrificed on the altar of imagined constraints? That question has been troubling me since last week, when I stepped out of the glitteringly art deco Grand Rex cinema in Paris. I had just been to see part one of La Bataille de Gaulle, a two-part epic based on British historian Julian Jackson’s extraordinary biogr

stories.title: European heat wave is not normal summer weather
stories.summary: Human-induced climate change has intensified temperatures, making this year's record heat wave up to 4 degrees Celsius hotter.

stories.title: Heat wave: Why you should skip that cold beer or cocktail
stories.summary: Summer, the FIFA World Cup and a cold beer. Sounds like a dream? Be careful, though; the combination of heat and alcohol can quickly take a very bad turn.

stories.title: How Big Tech is changing journalism
stories.summary: Technology companies are shaping how news is presented and digested, leaving media outlets little option but to comply. At DW's Global Media Forum in Bonn, participants discussed how to resist and adapt.

stories.title: Why Germans don't have air conditioning
stories.summary: Unlike the US or parts of Asia, many homes in Germany and northern Europe aren't equipped to deal with extreme heat. But as the world gets hotter, trends are changing.

stories.title: How countries live with earthquakes and build resilience
stories.summary: Earthquakes are a fact of nature, but living with earthquakes doesn't have to mean living in fear. DW takes a closer look at some countries showing how preparation, innovation and determination can save lives.

stories.title: Human rights expert Philippe Sands wins German Peace Prize
stories.summary: From advocating for the law of ecocide to representing Palestinian rights, the descendant of Holocaust survivors tirelessly fights against genocide and crimes against humanity.

stories.title: Germany: Racism is almost a daily reality for Muslims
stories.summary: Insults, discrimination, violence, deaths: Experts warn of growing anti-Muslim racism in Germany — and criticize policymakers.

stories.title: First whales killed as Iceland restarts commercial hunt
stories.summary: Iceland resumes commercial whaling after a two-year pause, with two fin whales processed at the Hvalur facility, reigniting controversy and protests.

stories.title: Portugal's World Cup victory sparks celebrations in Lisbon
stories.summary: Thousands of fans in Lisbon celebrated Portugal's 5-0 World Cup victory over Uzbekistan, with Cristiano Ronaldo scoring twice and becoming the first player to score in six different World Cup tournaments.

stories.title: IMF to closely monitor China’s economic growth amid trade tensions
stories.summary: An accurate measurement of China's digital economy is a priority for other powers competing with Beijing in fast-developing technologies.

stories.title: Catholics walk barefoot in mud ritual
stories.summary: Devotees in rice paddies cover themselves in mud and banana leaves for the Taong Putik festival, honouring St. John the Baptist with barefoot processions.

stories.title: Philippines bans video game played by alleged high school shooter
stories.summary: The rare school shooting that left three students killed and 20 others injured has shocked the country.

stories.title: Watch: Moment earthquakes hit airport and buildings in Caracas
stories.summary: Video filmed by BBC reporters, contributors and locals shows the aftermath, as frantic residents evacuate.

stories.title: This Indian state is trying to ensure no one grows old alone
stories.summary: Kerala is searching for new ways to care for its ageing population as migration leaves more elderly people living alone.

stories.title: Independent MPs launch new Australian centrist party
stories.summary: Zali Steggall and Allegra Spender say Community Strong Australia will offer citizens 'reason over rage'.

stories.title: Anthropic accuses Chinese rival Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities
stories.summary: The firm alleged that Alibaba used fraudulent accounts to access data from its Claude AI model.

stories.title: Hormuz confusion deepens as Iran and US clash over who controls it
stories.summary: Trump said Tehran had promised no tolls or charges in the strait, hours after Iran and Oman announced a joint permit system asserting their authority over the waterway. Rubio flew to Abu Dhabi to reassure Gulf allies, as analysts warned control over Hormuz would be a major strategic victory for Iran

stories.title: At least 164 killed and 971 injured in powerful Venezuela earthquakes
stories.summary: Residents of the capital Caracas were forced to evacuate their residences and seek shelter outdoors as the quakes, which struck less than 200 km from the capital, caused widespread damage.

stories.title: Mumbai monsoon floods streets and disrupts travel
stories.summary: Heavy monsoon rains flood Mumbai, submerging roads, disrupting traffic and forcing residents to wade through waterlogged streets in India’s financial hub.

stories.title: Trump says 'courageous' Zelenskyy is 'doing pretty well' in war with Russia
stories.summary: "You have to say he's courageous, he's got great equipment, he's got great men, he's got fighters," Trump said of Zelenskyy.

stories.title: Moment earthquake hits Venezuela, sending people fleeing in panic
stories.summary: Images showing cracked walls, collapsed ceiling panels and debris strewn across parts of Simón Bolívar International Airport highlighted the scale of damage after two powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela on 24 June 2026.

stories.title: Heatwave scorches Europe from London to Madrid as temperatures near 39C
stories.summary: A powerful heatwave swept across Europe, stretching from France to Spain and Belgium and pushing temperatures towards 39C, raising concerns about public safety and pressure on infrastructure.

stories.title: North Korean soldier suspected of defecting crosses demilitarised zone into South Korea
stories.summary: According to data from South Korea's Ministry of Unification, more than 34,000 North Koreans have defected to the South since its records began in 1998.

stories.title: Trump asks Congress for billions for Iran war, after tension with Republicans
stories.summary: But the budget faces an uphill battle as the president spars with some members of his own party over the issue.

stories.title: IBM hails new 'block of flats' design breakthrough for ultra tiny chips
stories.summary: IBM says it has created the world's first known chip tech below 1 nanometre - but it will be some time before it's ready for production.

stories.title: International community pledges aid to Venezuela after devastating earthquakes
stories.summary: The United States Geological Survey has estimated that the final death toll could be between 10,000 and 100,000 people.

stories.title: Ryanair says it will reluctantly not charge parents to sit next to children
stories.summary: The airline had typically charged adults a fee of £8 each way to sit with their young children.

stories.title: IRGC warns ships off Oman's corridor in Hormuz as Rubio draws line on tolls
stories.summary: The IRGC warned that any vessel navigating outside Tehran's designated routes was acting illegally, directly rejecting Oman's newly announced temporary corridor, as Rubio told a GCC meeting the US would not accept fees regardless of how they are framed.

stories.title: Moment French navy boards suspected Russian 'ghost fleet' tanker
stories.summary: The French navy boarded a tanker suspected of belonging to Russia's so-called "ghost fleet" in the Mediterranean, the fifth such operation since September 2025, as Paris steps up efforts to enforce sanctions linked to the war in Ukraine.
